prompt: Three-quarter close detail of a metal binder clip holding a stack of multi-page government-style permit application forms on a clipboard. The forms have the standard ruled lines, signature blocks, and check-box columns of a generic municipal building department application — but every printed line of text is intentionally pure visual texture, no legible characters, just rectangular ink blocks suggesting form fields. A faint embossed-style circular seal visible upper-left, no readable text inside. A blue ballpoint pen rests on top mid-signature. Sawdust on the surface beneath. Shot with a 50mm normal lens at three-quarter angle, soft overcast natural daylight from a workshop window, even gentle shadows. Slightly desaturated palette, a red Anjo-style ceramic mug edge in the corner of the frame as the only saturated color. Working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright. No readable text anywhere.
